title: Streaming and handle ergonomics for long-running operations
description:
# Streaming and handle ergonomics for long-running operations

## Purpose

Make long-running Plexus operations (evaluations, reports, dispatched procedures) usable from the orchestrating agent without requiring it to either (a) sit blocking for 45 minutes silently or (b) write polling loops by hand. Two complementary mechanisms.

## Mechanism 1: Streaming during a blocking call

For operations that finish in a "feel-tolerable" timeframe (< several minutes), the call blocks Lua execution but progress events flow up through the existing trace bridge to the MCP client.

- Reuse `plexus/cli/procedure/tactus_adapters/trace.py` `PlexusTraceSink` and `_PlexusTraceLogBridge` (already throttle-stream with `STREAM_UPDATE_MAX_INTERVAL_SECONDS` and `STREAM_UPDATE_MIN_CHARS_DELTA`).
- Cost events from Epic 4 also stream up the same channel.
- MCP client sees: "evaluation X: 234/1000 items processed, current AC1 0.81, $0.42 spent so far" without any new code.
- Cursor MCP supports streaming notifications; verify behavior in non-Cursor clients (Claude Desktop, Continue.dev) and degrade gracefully if needed.

## Mechanism 2: Handle-based async for very long operations

For operations measured in tens of minutes or hours, blocking is unfriendly:

```lua
local handle = plexus.evaluation.run{ ..., async = true }
-- handle is a small table { id, status_url, created_at }
-- Lua program continues, can do other work or save the handle and return
return { evaluation_handle = handle }
```

The orchestrator can come back later (in a subsequent `execute_tactus` call) and:

```lua
local result = plexus.handle.await{ id = "..." }
-- or
local status = plexus.handle.status{ id = "..." }
```

## State / session model decision (must be made in this epic)

Three options for how handles are addressable:

A. Handles are global / per-account, identified by ID, persist indefinitely.
B. Handles are per-MCP-session, expire when session ends.
C. Handles are per-account but tied to an explicit `session_id` from the MCP entry point.

Recommendation: B + C — per-MCP-session by default, with explicit session_id for cross-session continuity. Decide during this epic.

## Cancellation

- `plexus.handle.cancel{ id = ... }` requests cancellation; underlying dispatch propagates per Epic 4.
- If the parent's budget exhausts, in-flight handles are auto-cancelled.

## Estimate

1.5 weeks. Streaming-side work is mostly wiring existing trace bridge to MCP transport; handle work is the larger design surface.

## Dependencies

- Epic 4 (budget): cost events ride the same streaming channel.
- Epic 6 (`execute_tactus`): the MCP-side streaming primitives.
